## What is AntMyERP?

AntMyERP is an all-in-one ERP and CRM platform purpose-built for businesses that manage field technicians and service contracts — think IT AMC providers, CCTV installers, elevator maintenance firms, and copier dealers. It combines sales pipeline management, service ticket dispatch, asset tracking, inventory, HR, and invoicing in a single system built around the field service lifecycle.

## Who is it for?

Companies in equipment sales and service — particularly in the AMC (Annual Maintenance Contract) and IT infrastructure space — that are outgrowing spreadsheets and disconnected tools. It fits Indian and MENA market SMBs particularly well, though the platform serves global customers. Teams with 10–200 users who need to manage technician schedules, track spare parts, and generate AMC renewals.

## Strengths

- **Field service-native design** — the whole platform is built around technician dispatch, ticket resolution, and SLA tracking, not bolted on.
- **Technician mobile app** — field staff can receive jobs, update ticket status, log parts used, and capture signatures on mobile.
- **All-modules pricing** — $50/user/mo covers everything; no module-by-module upsells.
- **Asset and inventory tracking** — tracks customer-owned equipment, service history, and spare parts consumption together.
- **Automation gains** — users report 60% productivity improvements and 33% better on-time completion after switching.

## What to consider

- QuickBooks integration is limited — teams using QuickBooks for accounting will find sync gaps.
- The iOS app lags behind the browser version in functionality; Android users fare better.
- The $50/user price point is higher than general-purpose CRMs, which may be hard to justify for teams that need only basic CRM without field service.

## Bottom line

AntMyERP is a strong fit if your business revolves around field service, service contracts, and equipment maintenance. It is not a general-purpose CRM. If that niche matches, it replaces several tools at once and the all-inclusive pricing becomes a good deal.
